source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Observers in reference frame S see an explosion located at x_1 = 480 \, \mathrm{m}. A second explosion occurs 5 \, \mu \mathrm{s} later at x_2 = 1200 \, \mathrm{m}. In reference frame S', which is moving along the +x axis at speed v, the explosions occur at the same point in space. \ Calibrate the ct' axis and determine the separation in time in \mu \mathrm{s} between the two explosions as measured in S'. \

image_dependency: requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate non-text reliance in dataset annotation
An image asset exists and passed registry checks

However, the question text itself provides the key numerical quantities (x1, x2, time delay, moving frame condition) and does not explicitly reference a figure, so image dependence is not scored extremely high
multi_step: The task requires combining multiple given quantities: x1=480 m, x2=1200 m, and Δt=5 μs
To solve, one must first use the condition 'same point in space in S'' to infer the frame speed or equivalent constraint
Then one must compute the transformed time separation in S', so this is at least a two-step physics/mathematical reasoning problem
verifiability: answer_type=short_text with a single numeric result and unit ('4.39 μs')
Multiple-choice options are provided, giving an additional discrete check on the final result
The result is formula-checkable using standard special relativity transformations

decision_reasons: All core assets are present: question text, answer, and primary image all passed registry checks
The sample has high multi-step and verifiability scores, indicating strong candidate value
Although image dependency is flagged as ambiguous, the image asset is available and the text appears to contain key quantities, so there is no core semantic gap
